description: Rules governing supernatural power: how spells and powers are acquired, cast, fueled, and organized, what casting risks, and how divine and psychic power differ from arcane practice.

casting.description: How spells or powers are activated, fueled, and constrained during play.

focuses.spell_slots: Casters prepare or know spells and expend limited per-rest slots by level to cast them (D&D-style Vancian casting).
focuses.mana_points: Casting spends a numeric energy pool; bigger effects cost more points, and the pool recovers with rest or time (spell points, RuneQuest magic points).
focuses.freeform_improvised: Casters describe any effect within their power's theme and the system prices or adjudicates it on the fly rather than using a fixed spell list (Mage: the Ascension, Ars Magica spontaneous magic).
focuses.verbal_somatic_components: Spells require spoken words, gestures, or material components, so binding, gagging, or disarming a caster can prevent casting (D&D components).
focuses.skill_roll_casting: Casting is a skill test that can fail, with the roll's quality shaping the spell's strength or side effects (WFRP Channelling, Shadowrun Spellcasting).
focuses.ritual_casting: Lengthy ceremonies with extra time, participants, or ingredients cast spells beyond normal combat magic or without spending scarce resources (D&D 5e rituals, Call of Cthulhu rites).
focuses.at_will_cantrips: Minor spells can be cast without limit or cost, giving casters a magical baseline that never runs dry (D&D 5e cantrips).
focuses.fire_and_forget_memorization: Each cast spell vanishes from the caster's memory and must be re-memorized from a book during rest (AD&D Vancian memorization).
focuses.life_force_casting: Spells are fueled by the caster's own hit points, blood, or vitality, making every casting a wound (Shadowrun blood magic, cast-from-hit-points traditions in OSR play).
focuses.attribute_burn_casting: Casters may burn attribute points — temporarily or permanently — to empower a casting beyond its normal limits (DCC spellburn).
focuses.recharge_powers: Powers become unavailable after use and return on a die roll, timer, or scene boundary rather than from a spent pool (recharge abilities in D&D 4e/5e-style design).
focuses.concentration_limits: Sustained spells occupy the caster's concentration, capping how many can run at once and ending when concentration breaks (D&D 5e concentration).
focuses.counterspelling: Casters can react to enemy casting with an opposed check or resource spend to weaken or negate the incoming spell (D&D counterspell, Ars Magica certamen-style duels).
focuses.overchannel_push: A caster may deliberately exceed safe casting limits for extra power, accepting increased risk of harm or mishap (overchannelling in WFRP, pushing rolls in Forbidden Lands magic).
focuses.group_cooperative_casting: Multiple casters link their efforts to reach magnitudes none could achieve alone, pooling dice, points, or checks (Ars Magica's Wizard's Communion, Mage group rituals).

acquisition.description: How characters gain, learn, and expand magical capability.

focuses.spellbook_study: New spells are learned by studying, copying, and transcribing them from books and scrolls, making libraries treasure (D&D wizards, Ars Magica lab texts).
focuses.pact_granted: Power flows from a bargain with a patron entity whose favor, terms, and agenda bind the caster (D&D warlocks, Sorcerer demons).
focuses.bloodline_innate: Magic is inherited or inborn, surfacing as natural talent that is trained rather than studied (D&D sorcerers, Earthdawn adepts).
focuses.school_specialization: Casters commit to a school, tradition, or college that grants depth in one style while restricting or penalizing others (AD&D specialist wizards, WFRP colleges of magic).
focuses.spell_research: Casters invent new spells or variants through downtime research with time, money, and risk of failure (OSR spell research, Ars Magica lab work).
focuses.level_up_spell_picks: New spells arrive automatically as a class feature of gaining levels, chosen from a class list without in-fiction study (D&D sorcerer spell picks).
focuses.found_grimoires: Spells enter play as loot — captured grimoires, scrolls, and enemy spellbooks that must be deciphered before use (OSR play, MÖRK BORG scrolls).
focuses.mentor_apprenticeship: Magic is taught person-to-person by a master whose availability, price, and secrets gate what can be learned (Ars Magica apprenticeships, wuxia master-student traditions).
focuses.cult_initiation: Deeper magic is unlocked by initiation through the ranks of a mystery cult or secret society, tying power to membership and duty (RuneQuest rune cults).
focuses.xp_spell_purchase: Spells and powers are bought directly with earned experience or karma like any other advancement (Shadowrun, World of Darkness disciplines).

risk.description: The dangers, costs, and corrupting consequences of wielding magic.

focuses.corruption: Using magic gradually taints body or soul, accumulating a corruption score that brings mutations, moral decay, or damnation (WFRP Chaos corruption, Shadow of the Demon Lord).
focuses.mishap_tables: Failed or botched castings roll on tables of misfires, from embarrassing quirks to catastrophic disasters (DCC spell misfires, WFRP miscasts).
focuses.backlash_damage: Casting inflicts direct harm — fatigue, stun, or wounds — on the caster, scaled to the spell's power (Shadowrun drain, Earthdawn backlash).
focuses.paradox: Magic that violates consensual reality or is witnessed by unbelievers rebounds on the caster as reality pushes back (Mage: the Ascension Paradox).
focuses.doom_accumulation: Each casting or overreach feeds a rising doom pool or track whose eventual discharge empowers dire consequences (sorcery adding to the GM's Doom in Conan 2d20).
focuses.sanity_cost: Learning or casting spells erodes the caster's sanity, pricing magic in madness (Call of Cthulhu spell costs).
focuses.wild_magic_surges: Casting can trigger random uncontrolled magical effects unrelated to the intended spell (D&D wild magic surges).
focuses.unwanted_attention: Magic use draws the notice of spirits, demons, or watchful powers, accumulating supernatural attention with consequences (KULT, Unknown Armies-style occult exposure).
focuses.burnout_permanent_loss: Overreaching can permanently scar or destroy magical capacity, reducing or removing the gift itself (burnout in Shadowrun adept lore, Fading Suns theurgy loss).
focuses.magic_addiction: Casting is psychologically or physically addictive, requiring checks to resist overuse and escalating dependency (Shadowrun focus addiction, sorcery addiction in dark fantasy designs).
focuses.taboo_restrictions: Power is bound to personal taboos, geasa, or bans whose violation strips or weakens the magic (geasa in Celtic-flavored games, fae bans).

structure.description: How magic itself is organized, taxonomized, and made lawful within the setting's rules.

focuses.schools_of_magic: Spells are classified into schools or spheres — evocation, necromancy, illusion — that structure specialization, resistance, and access (D&D schools, Mage spheres).
focuses.spell_levels: Spells are ranked in numbered tiers of power that gate who can cast them and what they cost (D&D spell levels 1-9).
focuses.words_of_power: Magic is assembled from a limited vocabulary of potent words combined at need, trading fixed spells for a generative grammar (Pathfinder's Words of Power, true-speech traditions).
focuses.sympathetic_links: Magic targeting someone at a distance requires a sympathetic connection — hair, blood, a true likeness — whose quality scales the effect (Ars Magica arcane connections, Unknown Armies).
focuses.true_names: Knowing a being's true name grants coercive power over it, making names guarded secrets and summoning currency (classic demonology, Earthsea-derived designs like Invisible Sun).
focuses.technique_form_combos: Every spell is built by pairing a verb-like technique with a noun-like form, so a small grid of elements yields the whole of magic (Ars Magica's Creo Ignem grid).
focuses.power_sites_auras: Magical strength varies by location: auras, ley lines, and nodes amplify, dampen, or warp casting (Ars Magica auras, Mage nodes).
focuses.belief_paradigms: What magic can do is defined by the practitioner's paradigm or tradition, so different cultures' magic follows genuinely different rules (Mage: the Ascension paradigms).
focuses.runes_glyphs: Magic is encoded in physical runes, glyphs, or inscriptions that store effects in objects and places until triggered (RuneQuest runes, WFRP runesmiths).
focuses.spell_lists_by_class: Access to spells is partitioned into class- or tradition-specific lists, so who you are determines what magic you may ever touch (D&D arcane versus divine lists).
focuses.enchantment_item_crafting: Permanent magic is invested into items through lengthy crafting processes with material, time, and essence costs (Ars Magica lab enchantment, D&D magic item creation).

divine.description: Magic granted by gods and spirits, priced in faith, service, and obedience.

focuses.favor_economy: Divine power is a currency of favor earned through sacrifice, worship, and service, and spent to work the god's magic (RuneQuest Rune Points, piety pools in Mythras).
focuses.domain_powers: A deity's portfolio dictates the themes of magic granted, so priests of war, sea, and harvest wield visibly different powers (D&D cleric domains).
focuses.miracles: Beyond rote prayers, the truly faithful can petition for open-ended miracles adjudicated by the GM and priced in devotion (Godbound words, In Nomine interventions).
focuses.faith_tests: Working divine power requires testing the character's faith, with doubt or sin weakening the roll (Deadlands blessed, Dogs in the Vineyard-style conviction).
focuses.divine_intervention_pleas: In extremity a worshipper may plead for direct intervention on a long-odds roll, often at permanent cost (RuneQuest divine intervention, DCC clerics burning Luck).
focuses.sin_and_atonement: Violating the deity's code strips powers until formal penance or atonement restores standing (D&D paladin oaths, Pendragon religious virtues).
focuses.spirit_bargaining: Powers are obtained case by case by negotiating with individual spirits, each with its own price and personality (Shadowrun conjuring, Werewolf spirit gifts).

psionics.description: Powers of the mind, typically point-fueled, discipline-structured, and taxing to the wielder.

focuses.power_points: Psychic abilities draw on a personal reservoir of power points spent per manifestation and recovered with rest (D&D 3.5 psionics, Traveller psionic strength).
focuses.discipline_trees: Powers are organized into disciplines — telepathy, psychokinesis, clairsentience — advanced as separate trees or skill tracks (AD&D psionic disciplines, Babylon 5-style telepathy tiers).
focuses.psychic_strain: Manifesting powers inflicts fatigue, stress, or nonlethal harm on the psion, throttling consecutive use even when points remain (strain and feedback mechanics across psi-focused designs).
focuses.wild_talents: Rare untrained individuals manifest a single random psychic knack outside any formal discipline (AD&D 2e wild talents, Dark Sun).
focuses.psychic_duels: Mind-to-mind conflict is its own opposed subsystem with attack and defense modes distinct from physical combat (AD&D psychic contest modes, telepathic combat in Traveller-adjacent play).
focuses.power_augmentation: A manifested power's range, damage, or targets scale with extra points spent at casting time, letting one power flex across levels (D&D 3.5 psionic augmentation).
